title: Any Random drops
description: This is a mod that fills Minecraft with uncertainty. Breaking blocks, crafting items, and killing mobs will all result in completely random drops.
body:
🎲 Random Drop
A Fabric mod that brings ultimate unpredictability to Minecraft. It completely overhauls the game’s drop and crafting logic, turning every block break, mob kill, and crafting attempt into a massive gamble!

✨ Core Features
⛏️ Random Block Drops
Say goodbye to traditional mining! Breaking any block can drop absolutely anything. Mining stone drops a diamond? Digging dirt yields Netherite? Everything is possible, and anything can drop.

☠️ Random Mob Drops
Mobs no longer drop their standard loot. Creepers dropping Enchanted Books instead of Gunpowder? Zombies dropping Elytra? Every kill is an absolute surprise (or a terrifying shock)!

🔧 Random Crafting Results
The crafting table is no longer reliable. Throw in some Cobblestone, and you might just get an Elytra out! Turn your crafting table into a literal loot box machine.

⚙️ Highly Customizable
Don’t want the game to become completely unbeatable? No problem, the mod offers extremely flexible control:

Dual Configuration: Supports the traditional random-drop.json file editing, and now features an in-game GUI config screen in the latest update!
Independent Toggles: Block drops, mob drops, crafting randomness, natural drops, leaf decay… all features can be toggled on or off individually at any time in-game.
Blacklist Filtering: Add items to a blacklist to precisely exclude them from the random pool (e.g., crucial progression items or specific keys).
📝1.1.0 Latest Changelog
🆕 New Features

In-Game Config Menu: No more editing files! You can now open a configuration screen directly in-game to toggle random features on or off whenever you want.
More Randomness Sources: Natural block drops (like cacti) and leaf decay drops are now part of the random pool. These specific features can also be toggled independently.
🐛 Bug Fixes

Fixed an issue where Shift + Left-Click quick crafting did not trigger random drops.
Fixed a bug where random drops would still incorrectly trigger when a vanilla block originally had an empty drop (e.g., Bedrock).
⚠️ Warning: This mod makes the game highly unpredictable. It is strongly recommended to use it on a new world or with a backed-up save. Perfect for challenging yourself, or absolutely trolling your friends on a multiplayer server!


Installation
Install Fabric Loader ≥ 0.19.2
Install Fabric API
Download the latest jar and place it in your mods
Launch the game and embrace the chaos
Supported Versions
Minecraft 1.21.11
